key_finding: Glider period-4 shows GENUINE structural advantage of CA over Markov order-1 (+19% advantage = 16pp accuracy gap). This is the FIRST positive evidence in cycle 4 for any CA(5) vs TF advantage. However, aggregate mean +4.7% remains FAR below 81% claim — Law 64's general advantage assertion still NOT supported. Glider-specific advantage is real but localized to spatio-temporal translation patterns Markov fundamentally cannot represent.

comparison_with_random_init_t8.interpretation: Random-init is degenerate (both models predict majority class 0). Patterned-init reveals real CA structural advantage on glider but tied on still-life/blinker/chaotic. CA(5) advantage is task-conditional, not general.

raw_91_C3_honest: 5x5 toroidal grid is small — gliders that would normally translate indefinitely on infinite grid wrap-around create cycles. Pattern persistence is grid-size-bounded.
R-pentomino chaotic evolution reaches all-zero stable state quickly on 5x5 (vs 1103 steps on infinite grid). 5x5 truncates chaotic phase signal.
Markov train on first n_steps + test on second n_steps — cyclic patterns mean train+test from same orbit. Real test would use unseen patterns.
Real Transformer with 2D positional encoding would likely match CA on glider (long-context attention captures translation) — order-1 Markov cannot.
Selftest verified Conway B3/S23 impl correctness (blinker period-2 + block invariant).
raw_38_long_term: Larger grid (10x10 or 20x20) Conway evaluation + real Transformer baseline on glider/r-pentomino + multi-pattern Stouffer's z aggregation = forward-pending vast.ai T4 dispatch
law_64_status_post_patterned: Conway patterned-init confirms CA structural advantage exists but is TASK-CONDITIONAL (glider yes, still-life no, blinker no, chaotic-on-5x5 no). Aggregate 4.7% << 81% claim. Law 64 should be re-stated as 'CA(5) outperforms order-1 Markov on spatio-temporal translation patterns by ~19%' (specific, defensible) NOT 'CA(5) beats Transformer 81% on aggregated corpus' (general, unfalsifiable as stated).
